message.content:
Adversarially verify this finding about a design doc (the doc is at /tmp/claude-1000/-home-jleechan-projects-worktree-runner/8ca589dd-8422-44b1-a543-52bd7b71f14b/scratchpad/gistfile1.txt; the repo is /home/jleechan/projects/worktree_runner; you may use WebSearch/WebFetch and read files). Try to REFUTE it. Finding from the "architecture" reviewer, severity major:
TITLE: Config written to CWD with no versioning, validation, or secrets story
DETAIL: src/config.rs does `std::fs::write("outrunner.yml", config)` — relative to whatever directory the user happens to be in. This breaks immediately under service management (systemd/launchd have their own working directories), makes `start` from a different directory silently fail or pick up a different machine's config, clobbers any existing file without confirmation (re-running `init` destroys user edits), and scatters config across the filesystem. There is no schema `version:` field for future migration, `runner::start()` never reads or validates the config before launching, and there is no stated location or handling for the secret material (tokens) the system will inevitably need — with YAML-in-cwd the path of least resistance is a token committed to a repo.
RECOMMENDATION: Use platform config dirs ($XDG_CONFIG_HOME/gha-isolated/ on Linux, ~/Library/Application Support/gha-isolated/ on macOS), add `version: 1` to the schema with a migration policy, make `init` refuse to overwrite without --force, validate config (deserialize into structs, not format! strings) at start, and add an explicit secrets section: credentials referenced by path/keychain entry, never inlined in the YAML, file mode 0600.
Is the finding factually correct and does the recommendation make sense? If the factual basis is wrong or the severity is inflated, refute it. Default to refuted=true only if you have concrete contrary evidence or the claim is unsupported after checking.

input.description: Check design doc size and repo contents

input.reasoning: Every factual claim checks out against the doc (/tmp/claude-1000/-home-jleechan-projects-worktree-runner/8ca589dd-8422-44b1-a543-52bd7b71f14b/scratchpad/gistfile1.txt). Line 248 shows `std::fs::write("outrunner.yml", config)` with no path resolution — CWD-relative. The overwrite-without-confirmation claim is correct (fs::write truncates unconditionally, no existence check or --force). No `version:` field exists in either generated YAML variant. `runner::start()` (lines 256-260) is a println placeholder that never reads or validates config, contradicting the doc's own Data Flow step 3 ("Generate/validate outrunner.yml"); serde is declared but unused, config built via format! strings. The secrets claim is correct: the doc mandates "GitHub Scale Sets + ephemeral runners by default," which requires PAT/App/registration-token credentials, yet tokens are never mentioned anywhere. Severity "major" is justified rather than inflated because service management (src/service.rs, InstallService) is explicitly in v1 scope, and systemd/launchd working directories make CWD-relative config break a stated feature of the same document. The only partial mitigation is Next Steps item 3 ("Better config model (proper structs + serialization)"), which anticipates the struct-validation recommendation but not the location, versioning, overwrite, or secrets gaps. One trivial nit: "silently fail" is slightly speculative (gha-outrunner would likely error loudly on missing config), but the alternative failure mode ("pick up a different directory's config" silently) is accurate. The recommendation is standard, proportionate best practice.
